subject: PhD Topology
instructions: Work problems 1–5 and show all work. Support all statements to the best of your ability. Answer problems 6–15 with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problems.text: Can a compact space contain a discrete infinite subspace?
Prove that the product of two connected spaces is connected.
Give an example of a space which is path connected but not locally connected.
Show that the Klein bottle with a deleted closed disk \(K\setminus D\) is not a retract of \(K\).
Prove that the group \(SO(n)\) is compact in the matrix topology.
State the Lefschetz fixed point theorem.
Construct a map \(f:S^3\to S^3\) of degree 3.
Prove that for a path connected topological group \(G\) the Euler characteristic \(\chi(G)\) is zero.
Does every function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{R}\) admit a continuous extension \(\bar f:\beta\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{R}\) to the Stone–Čech compactification?
What can you say about the \(k\)-homology groups of a closed orientable \(n\)-manifold for
Give a definition of the compact-open topology for the space of functions.
Let \(j:S^1\subset S^4\) be an embedding. Can \(\pi_1(S^4\setminus j(S^1))\) be trivial? If it is not trivial, can the fundamental group \(\pi_1(S^4\setminus j(S^1))\) be abelian?
Give a definition of the wedge of a family of topological spaces. Prove that an infinite wedge of circles is not metrizable.
